title: Will Gets Committed

jokes.text: Shabba!
jokes.setup: Opening scene energy
jokes.punchline: Will's signature exclamation establishing his street persona
jokes.explanation: Pure character comedy - Will's catchphrase immediately establishes his street swagger in contrast to the Bel-Air setting

jokes.text: It's kind of fly, right? Don't you wish you lived on TV?
jokes.setup: Will acknowledging the audience/TV format
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall about living on television
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor that works on multiple levels - acknowledging the show's premise while being character-appropriate for Will

jokes.text: Must be what it sounds like to wake up in Little Richard's crib.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil waking everyone up loudly at 6 AM
jokes.punchline: Comparing the noise to Little Richard's flamboyant lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor with a perfect pop culture reference that fits Will's voice

jokes.text: She dumps you, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Carlton wondering how his dream ends
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt, dream-crushing response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character work - Will's casual cruelty toward Carlton delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: Step to it, Ash.
jokes.setup: Will suggesting someone should confront Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Immediately delegating to little Ashley when Phil appears
jokes.explanation: Classic Will cowardice - all talk until authority shows up, then passes the buck to a child

jokes.text: Night, Sister Mary Margaret.
jokes.setup: Hilary coming home late at night
jokes.punchline: Pretending she's been with a nun to cover her activities
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Hilary's ditzy attempts at deception, though the payoff is predictable

jokes.text: Sister Mary Margaret drives a Porsche?
jokes.setup: The nun reference from Hilary
jokes.punchline: Questioning the lifestyle of this supposed nun
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation that exposes the holes in Hilary's story with perfect economy

jokes.text: Man, they must got it going on down at the convent, huh?
jokes.setup: Following up on the Porsche observation
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the convent is unexpectedly wealthy
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the joke about the suspicious nun story

jokes.text: We're not Catholic. Well, is my face red. Good night.
jokes.setup: Being caught in her nun lie
jokes.punchline: Casually acknowledging the mistake and trying to escape
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - caught red-handed but still trying to maintain dignity with casual dismissal

jokes.text: I also said Daddy lost weight.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil reminding her she agreed to help
jokes.punchline: Pointing out she also lied about his weight loss
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that deflects while simultaneously insulting Uncle Phil - very Hilary

jokes.text: Look, kids, this is where we lived when we first got married. And this is the little park where we walked little Hilary. And, look, there's the pet store where we bought little Carlton.
jokes.setup: Mocking Uncle Phil and Aunt Viv's nostalgia
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Carlton was purchased at a pet store
jokes.explanation: Great escalating joke structure with perfect Will-style roasting of Carlton's weird behavior

jokes.text: I think it's cute. They're going to be mushy and lovey-dovey all day.
jokes.setup: Will's cynical take on their parents' nostalgia
jokes.punchline: Carlton's genuine appreciation for romance, setting up dramatic irony
jokes.explanation: Sets up dramatic irony since we're about to see their parents fighting - gains meaning on rewatch

jokes.text: Vivian, what the hell is the matter with you?
jokes.setup: Carlton just said they'll be lovey-dovey
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to them fighting
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - immediate contradiction of Carlton's optimism with marital tension

jokes.text: Happy anniversary, sweetheart. It's not our anniversary, Philip. Oh, God, good.
jokes.setup: Phil trying to guess what he forgot
jokes.punchline: His relief when it's not their anniversary
jokes.explanation: Relatable married couple humor - the panic and relief of potentially forgotten occasions

jokes.text: Vivian, don't you think you're being just a wee bit erratic this morning?
jokes.setup: Viv's mood swings and complaints
jokes.punchline: Phil's diplomatic understatement of 'wee bit erratic'
jokes.explanation: Good character work for Phil's lawyer-like diplomatic language when dealing with his wife's mood

jokes.text: Well, actually, Aunt Viv, you got a kind of a Don King... Kris Kross, Bride of Frankenthing going.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv asking Will's opinion on her hair
jokes.punchline: Will's brutally honest description mixing multiple pop culture references
jokes.explanation: Great Will moment - mixing pop culture references into an accidentally devastating insult

jokes.text: Not many people could pull it off. You know, as you're proving, right, but...
jokes.setup: Trying to soften the hair insult
jokes.punchline: Accidentally making it worse by suggesting she can't pull it off either
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will character moment - trying to be diplomatic but making it infinitely worse

jokes.text: Shooting baskets with Michael Jordon.
jokes.setup: Phil asking where she was yesterday
jokes.punchline: Viv's sarcastic non-answer
jokes.explanation: Solid deadpan delivery showing Viv's frustration with Phil's interrogation

jokes.text: You know, Clair would never talk to Cliff like that.
jokes.setup: Witnessing his parents' marital tension
jokes.punchline: Referencing The Cosby Show characters as the ideal marriage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-reference to NBC's other family sitcom, perfectly in character for Carlton's TV-influenced worldview

jokes.text: Yo, my brother, when the going gets tough, the tough gets going.
jokes.setup: Will trying to be motivational to a community worker
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of the famous saying
jokes.explanation: Standard malapropism joke - not particularly original but fits Will's character

jokes.text: It's Hammer time.
jokes.setup: Continuing his motivational speech
jokes.punchline: Random MC Hammer reference
jokes.explanation: Lazy pop culture reference without much craft - relies on the reference itself for the humor

jokes.text: Sorry about that, man, you know, you have to excuse my cousin... you know, he's an idiot.
jokes.setup: Carlton doing something embarrassing
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt assessment to a stranger
jokes.explanation: Classic Will-Carlton dynamic - Will's casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Oh, God, what a stallion. Time to saddle up.
jokes.setup: Carlton seeing an attractive woman
jokes.punchline: His awkward horse metaphor for approaching her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton cringe moment - trying to be smooth but coming off as weird and awkward

jokes.text: Yo, what's up, baby? Hurry up, write your number down before I don't want it no more.
jokes.setup: Will trying to pick up the same woman
jokes.punchline: His overconfident, time-pressured approach
jokes.explanation: Good Will swagger moment - the arrogance is both attractive and off-putting simultaneously

jokes.text: And I'm going to buy you a car.
jokes.setup: Will making his move on Simone
jokes.punchline: Carlton's desperate attempt to one-up him with money
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the cousin rivalry - Carlton's naive attempt to use wealth as charm

jokes.text: Carlton can't help you, he's on the debate team.
jokes.setup: Simone needing physical help
jokes.punchline: Will dismissing Carlton by highlighting his nerdiness
jokes.explanation: Solid character work - Will using Carlton's academic achievements as an insult

jokes.text: Really, I'm captain of my debate team.
jokes.setup: Will trying to make Carlton seem uncool
jokes.punchline: Carlton proudly confirming and amplifying the nerdiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton obliviousness - completely missing that this makes him less attractive

jokes.text: How about over there somewhere, like, say, Cleveland?
jokes.setup: Will asking where to take some lumber
jokes.punchline: Simone suggesting he take it (and himself) very far away
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that deflates Will's cockiness with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Tell me to go to Cleveland, mushroom head.
jokes.setup: Being told off by Simone
jokes.punchline: Will's wounded ego response with weak insult
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's immaturity when his smooth act fails - childish name-calling

jokes.text: No, row versus wade, the best way to cross a stream.
jokes.setup: Simone assuming Roe v. Wade is about reproductive rights
jokes.punchline: Carlton's literal interpretation as a debate about crossing water
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that works on multiple levels - Carlton's innocence, clever pun, and social commentary

jokes.text: Predictable, sir? She said that I wear the same... Blue shirt every weekend, sir? Yes, I mean, just because a man has... Froot Loops every morning. Just because I watch... Legal Eagles every night.
jokes.setup: Phil denying he's predictable
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey finishing all his sentences, proving he's extremely predictable
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalating structure where Geoffrey's ability to predict Phil's every word perfectly proves Viv's point

jokes.text: Why, you're a regular hotbed of spontaneity.
jokes.setup: Phil asking if he's predictable
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's perfectly sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey sarcasm - polite on the surface but devastatingly cutting underneath

jokes.text: Because your soda's flat.
jokes.setup: Hilary wondering why no one wants her soda
jokes.punchline: The obvious reason delivered with total obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Simple Hilary moment showing her disconnection from basic cause and effect

jokes.text: And then when I was 12, I played Maria... in an all-boy production of West Side Story.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to impress Simone
jokes.punchline: Sharing an embarrassing childhood story as if it's impressive
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Carlton completely misreading what makes someone attractive

jokes.text: Will you marry me?
jokes.setup: Carlton's increasingly nerdy oversharing
jokes.punchline: Simone's sarcastic proposal in response to his 'charm'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of sarcasm - two words that completely deflate Carlton's earnest courtship

jokes.text: Well, there go the nerdyweds.
jokes.setup: Carlton and Simone walking off together
jokes.punchline: Will's portmanteau combining 'nerdy' and 'newlyweds'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both funny and shows Will's jealousy masked as mockery

jokes.text: Don't... I'm not finished with you yet, woman. Well, I'm through with you, man. Yeah, that's right, I'm bad.
jokes.setup: Phil trying to assert marital authority
jokes.punchline: Viv one-upping him and walking away triumphantly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the power dynamics in their marriage reversal

jokes.text: You're not coming back.
jokes.setup: Will making excuses about future commitments
jokes.punchline: Noah cutting through the BS with blunt truth
jokes.explanation: Cuts through polite social fiction with uncomfortable truth - moment turns serious

jokes.text: Yo, I'm from Philly. We had to save up to be poor.
jokes.setup: Noah accusing Will of being privileged
jokes.punchline: Will's comeback about his humble origins
jokes.explanation: Excellent line that's both funny and reveals character depth - defending his street cred with humor

jokes.text: Tell Dan Quayle I said, 'What's up.'
jokes.setup: Noah dismissing Will as privileged
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to the famously out-of-touch Vice President
jokes.explanation: Smart political reference that lands the insult about Will being out of touch with reality

jokes.text: I don't even know Dan Quayle.
jokes.setup: Noah's Dan Quayle insult
jokes.punchline: Will's defensive response missing the point
jokes.explanation: Shows Will missing the point of the insult - good character work about his defensive nature

jokes.text: Make up your mind, man. What do you want me to do first?
jokes.setup: Being given contradictory commands during the robbery
jokes.punchline: Hector's practical question in the middle of chaos
jokes.explanation: Good tension-breaking moment - practical response to illogical situation

jokes.text: Dad, don't do anything stupid, you haven't updated your will yet.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil confronting the robbery situation
jokes.punchline: Carlton's practical concern about inheritance
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's perfectly in character - Carlton's materialistic priorities even in crisis

jokes.text: Yeah, ain't you the guy from Family Matters?
jokes.setup: Recognizing Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Confusing him with another large Black TV character
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about Black TV representation, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Curly? They must go way back.
jokes.setup: Hearing Uncle Phil's old nickname
jokes.punchline: Will's assessment of their long history
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about the obvious nickname for a bald man

jokes.text: Who's Hector? I don't know, but be nice to him... he's got a bat.
jokes.setup: Not knowing who Hector is
jokes.punchline: Carlton's practical advice about the armed man
jokes.explanation: Good practical humor in a tense situation - Carlton's survival instincts

jokes.text: But, of course, who else could it be with those muy grandes orejas?
jokes.setup: Recognizing Will as little Willie
jokes.punchline: Referencing Will's distinctive big ears in Spanish
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that adds authenticity to their shared history

jokes.text: What does that mean? Big ears.
jokes.setup: Will not understanding the Spanish
jokes.punchline: Carlton's blunt translation
jokes.explanation: Simple translation joke that lands because of Carlton's matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: Hector, let me see that bat for a second.
jokes.setup: Being insulted about his big ears
jokes.punchline: Will's violent impulse toward his elder
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's hot temper when his appearance is mocked - good character consistency

jokes.text: I'll remember him now!
jokes.setup: Being told to remember Hector from childhood
jokes.punchline: Carlton's enthusiastic response after being threatened
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - missing social cues and being overly enthusiastic at wrong times

jokes.text: Hector, I love what you've done with the place.
jokes.setup: Seeing their old apartment is now a store
jokes.punchline: Hilary's polite but inappropriate interior design comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary obliviousness - treating a grocery store like a home renovation

jokes.text: He ain't doing that well.
jokes.setup: Hector asking about being paid back for Ring Dings
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt assessment of Uncle Phil's generosity
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Uncle Phil's success - Will keeping him humble

jokes.text: Look, there's Carlton.
jokes.setup: Looking at height marks on the wall from childhood
jokes.punchline: Carlton's mark is presumably very low/short
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Carlton's height that the audience would see on the wall

jokes.text: Don't even think about kissing me.
jokes.setup: Emotional moment about family love in the old apartment
jokes.punchline: Will's defensive response to Uncle Phil's emotional state
jokes.explanation: Classic Will deflection of emotional moments with humor - protective mechanism

jokes.text: Well, I tried flushing her down the toilet, but the little creep floats.
jokes.setup: Ashley throwing her doll in garbage
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact explanation of attempted doll murder
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor from a child - the casual violence described in innocent terms

jokes.text: What have you been smoking?
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv's speech about things more important than money
jokes.punchline: Ashley's incredulous response
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of expected wholesome response - Ashley's materialistic shock

jokes.text: Word up, and she was divorced, too.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil saying the woman was old enough to be his mother
jokes.punchline: Will pointing out she was also available
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's teenage priorities - missing the point about appropriateness

jokes.text: Parents just don't understand.
jokes.setup: Being lectured by Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Reference to Will Smith's hit song
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-reference to Will Smith's real rap career - perfect integration of actor and character

jokes.text: No, The Wall Street Journal, the part where AMC goes belly up.
jokes.setup: Will offering to read children's books
jokes.punchline: Carlton wanting financial news as bedtime reading
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton characterization - child who finds corporate bankruptcy soothing

jokes.text: What, tall?
jokes.setup: Carlton saying he knows what he wants to be when he grows up
jokes.punchline: Will's cruel but accurate observation
jokes.explanation: Sharp timing and delivery - Will's instinctive roast of Carlton's height

jokes.text: I'm black?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil mentioning the first black president
jokes.punchline: Carlton's apparent ignorance of his own race
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment that perfectly captures Carlton's disconnection from Black culture - absurd yet believable for the character

jokes.text: I'm rich! I'm rich!
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil getting called back for job interview
jokes.punchline: Carlton's immediate materialistic celebration
jokes.explanation: Pure Carlton moment - immediately thinking about personal financial gain

jokes.text: Did I ever tell you folks how much I like visiting?
jokes.setup: Talk of moving closer to rich neighborhoods
jokes.punchline: Will's strategic positioning for permanent residency
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's opportunistic nature - always thinking about his own advantage

jokes.text: Man, I wonder if that divorced lady still live here? She probably like 127 or something...
jokes.setup: Nostalgic moment in old apartment
jokes.punchline: Will still thinking about his romantic prospects with the elderly woman
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier joke, showing Will's persistent inappropriate interests

jokes.text: Great! I'm going to plan a party.
jokes.setup: Learning the house will be empty next week
jokes.punchline: Hilary's immediate party planning
jokes.explanation: Standard Hilary moment - thinking only of social opportunities

jokes.text: Of course not. Lucky for you.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking if she was with another man
jokes.punchline: Viv's threatening response
jokes.explanation: Good tension and release - Viv's mock-threatening response to Phil's jealousy

jokes.text: Philip, I'm pregnant!
jokes.setup: All the mystery about where she was and her strange behavior
jokes.punchline: The reveal that explains everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff - all her erratic behavior suddenly makes sense

tmdb_overview: When the Banks family returns to its former neighborhood to help old friends after a destructive city-wide riot, Vivian reveals a surprise to Philip.
